channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Break Into Tech AI Developer Scholarship: Your Pathway Into the Future of AI Development
item.dc:creator: Sarah Kayiwa
item.description:
The Break Into Tech AI Developer Scholarship—powered by Skillcrush by PowerToFly and funded by the Zero Irving Workforce Development Project Fund—is a transformative opportunity designed to open doors for aspiring AI developers from all backgrounds. With the tech industry evolving rapidly and AI reshaping every sector, this scholarship provides a rare chance for beginners and […]

The post Break Into Tech AI Developer Scholarship: Your Pathway Into the Future of AI Development appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Break Into Tech AI Developer Scholarship—powered by Skillcrush by PowerToFly and funded by the Zero Irving Workforce Development Project Fund—is a transformative opportunity designed to open doors for aspiring AI developers from all backgrounds. With the tech industry evolving rapidly and AI reshaping every sector, this scholarship provides a rare chance for beginners and career changers to gain the skills, confidence, and support needed to enter the world of AI development.

Skillcrush, the educational arm of PowerToFly, has already trained over 200,000 students across 148 countries, making it one of the most accessible and inclusive tech education platforms globally. This scholarship continues that mission by offering 15 fully supported spots for learners who want to break into tech—no prior experience required.

What This Scholarship Offers

The Break Into Tech AI Developer Scholarship is designed to give learners a comprehensive, beginner‑friendly pathway into AI development. Selected applicants will receive:

1. AI Development Training

Participants will gain foundational and advanced skills in AI development, including:

  • Understanding AI and machine learning concepts
  • Working with AI tools and frameworks
  • Building AI‑powered applications
  • Applying AI skills to real‑world projects

2. Job Search Support

Skillcrush’s career support team provides:

  • Personalized job search guidance
  • Resume and portfolio development
  • Interview preparation
  • Access to hiring networks

3. Beginner‑Friendly Learning Environment

Skillcrush is known for its supportive, flexible, and inclusive learning model. This scholarship is ideal for:

  • Complete beginners
  • Career changers
  • Individuals returning to the workforce
  • Anyone passionate about AI and tech

Why Skillcrush?

Skillcrush has built a global reputation for making tech education accessible, practical, and empowering. As part of PowerToFly, a leading platform for diverse talent in tech, Skillcrush combines industry expertise with a mission‑driven approach.

Key strengths include:

  • A proven track record of training 200,000+ students
  • A global community across 148 countries
  • Courses designed for real‑world job readiness
  • A commitment to diversity, equity, and inclusion in tech

How to Apply

The application process is simple and takes only 3 minutes to complete. Applicants must submit their information through the official application form to be considered.

Important Details

  • Number of spots available: 15
  • Application deadline: August 1, 2026
  • Eligibility: Beginners welcome; no prior tech experience required

Why You Should Apply

This scholarship is more than just a learning opportunity—it’s a launchpad into one of the fastest‑growing fields in the world. With AI transforming industries from healthcare to finance to creative arts, the demand for AI developers continues to rise. This program equips you with the skills and support needed to confidently enter the job market.

Whether you’re starting fresh or pivoting careers, this scholarship offers:

  • High‑quality training
  • Career support
  • A supportive learning community
  • A chance to join the future of tech

Start Your Application Today

If you’re ready to take the next step toward a career in AI development, now is the time. With only 15 spots available, early applications are encouraged.

Apply before August 1, 2026 to be considered.

For more information about this opportunity and to apply visit here

Discover more scholarship opportunities here 

 

The post Break Into Tech AI Developer Scholarship: Your Pathway Into the Future of AI Development appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Young NCB Advisory Group: Empowering Young Voices to Shape a Better Future for Children and Young People in the UK
item.dc:creator: Sarah Kayiwa
item.description:
The Young NCB Advisory Group (YNCB) is a dynamic and inclusive platform created by the National Children’s Bureau (NCB) to ensure that children and young people have a meaningful voice in shaping the policies, programmes, and decisions that affect their lives. This advisory group brings together passionate young people who want to speak out, influence […]

The post Young NCB Advisory Group: Empowering Young Voices to Shape a Better Future for Children and Young People in the UK appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Young NCB Advisory Group (YNCB) is a dynamic and inclusive platform created by the National Children’s Bureau (NCB) to ensure that children and young people have a meaningful voice in shaping the policies, programmes, and decisions that affect their lives. This advisory group brings together passionate young people who want to speak out, influence national conversations, and contribute to improving the lives of children and young people across the United Kingdom.

YNCB members play a central role in NCB’s governance, research, and policy work. Their insights, lived experiences, and perspectives help guide the organisation’s projects, ensuring that young people remain at the heart of every initiative.

What Is the Young NCB Advisory Group?

The Young NCB Advisory Group is a youth-led body that provides advice, feedback, and direction to the National Children’s Bureau. It is designed to amplify the voices of young people and ensure they are actively involved in shaping national work related to children’s rights, wellbeing, and development.

YNCB members contribute to:

  • Governance discussions
  • Research projects
  • Policy development
  • Programme design and evaluation
  • Strategic decision-making

The group serves as a safe, supportive, and empowering community where young people can express their views, share experiences, and collaborate with peers and professionals.

Who Can Join YNCB?

YNCB is committed to inclusivity and welcomes young people from diverse backgrounds. Membership is open to:

  • Young people aged 11–18
  • Young people aged 11–24 who have a disability or special educational need

No prior experience is required. The only expectation is a willingness to speak up, share ideas, and contribute to improving the lives of children and young people.

What Does Being a YNCB Member Involve?

Membership in the Young NCB Advisory Group offers a rich and rewarding experience. Young people participate in a variety of activities designed to build confidence, develop skills, and create real impact.

Key Responsibilities and Activities

  1. Attend Six Meetings Per Year Meetings take place both online and in-person at NCB’s London offices.
  2. Engage in Governance and Policy Work Members provide insights that help shape national programmes and organisational decisions.
  3. Participate in Research and Consultations Young people contribute to studies and consultations on issues affecting children and young people.
  4. Represent NCB in Public Spaces Members may speak at events, engage with professionals, and share their perspectives with decision-makers.
  5. Support Programme Development Members help shape youth-focused projects and initiatives.

Benefits of Joining the Young NCB Advisory Group

YNCB offers a wide range of benefits designed to support personal growth, leadership development, and meaningful participation.

  • No cost to participate — all travel and expenses are fully covered.
  • Vouchers provided for taking part in activities.
  • Opportunities to influence national work on issues affecting children and young people.
  • Skill development, including public speaking, teamwork, leadership, and communication.
  • Access to exclusive opportunities, such as:
    • Leading workshops for professionals
    • Recording podcasts
    • Interviewing new staff
    • Speaking with Members of Parliament
  • A supportive and inclusive environment that accommodates accessibility, travel, and dietary needs.
  • A chance to build friendships, join a community, and collaborate with like‑minded young people.

Why YNCB Matters

The Young NCB Advisory Group ensures that young people are not just consulted but are actively involved in shaping the systems and structures that impact their lives. Their voices influence national conversations, guide organisational priorities, and help create a more equitable and supportive environment for children and young people across the UK.

 

Deadline: Monday 6 July

Apply here

For more information about this opportunity visit here

Discover more fellowship opportunities here

 

The post Young NCB Advisory Group: Empowering Young Voices to Shape a Better Future for Children and Young People in the UK appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Digital Transformation in the Public Sector: A Comprehensive Training Programme by The Hague Academy for Local Governance
item.dc:creator: Sarah Kayiwa
item.description:
Digital transformation has become a defining priority for governments worldwide. As public institutions navigate rapid technological change, the need for strategic leadership, institutional readiness, and citizen‑centred innovation has never been more urgent. The Hague Academy for Local Governance addresses this global demand through its intensive, practice‑oriented course: Digital Transformation in the Public Sector, taking place […]

The post Digital Transformation in the Public Sector: A Comprehensive Training Programme by The Hague Academy for Local Governance appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Digital transformation has become a defining priority for governments worldwide. As public institutions navigate rapid technological change, the need for strategic leadership, institutional readiness, and citizen‑centred innovation has never been more urgent. The Hague Academy for Local Governance addresses this global demand through its intensive, practice‑oriented course: Digital Transformation in the Public Sector, taking place from 16–20 November 2026 in The Hague, Netherlands.

This in‑depth programme equips public sector professionals with the knowledge, tools, and leadership skills required to design, implement, and sustain digital reforms that enhance governance, transparency, and citizen engagement.

Course Overview

Governments increasingly recognise the transformative potential of digitalisation. However, true digital transformation extends far beyond adopting new technologies. It requires:

  • Institutional capacity
  • Policy coherence
  • Ethical data governance
  • Organisational change management
  • Cross‑sector collaboration
  • A culture of innovation

This course supports professionals from government institutions, development organisations, and innovation units to understand and shape digital transformation in their own contexts. Through expert‑led sessions, peer learning, and field visits to pioneering Dutch institutions, participants explore how digitalisation can strengthen governance, improve public services, and foster citizen participation.

What Participants Will Learn

The programme covers a wide range of themes essential for modern public administration, including:

  • Organisational change management
  • Responsible integration of Artificial Intelligence
  • Data‑driven decision‑making
  • Public data ethics
  • Cybersecurity resilience
  • Governance models for digital transformation
  • Citizen‑centred service design
  • Institutional culture and innovation

Participants engage in hands‑on exercises, real‑world case studies, and collaborative action planning to apply these concepts to their own institutions.

Key Learning Objectives

By the end of the course, participants will be able to:

  • Conduct a digital maturity assessment of their organisation and identify priority areas for improvement.
  • Integrate policy considerations for designing inclusive, accessible, and citizen‑responsive digital public services.
  • Apply change management strategies to lead digital reform within public institutions.
  • Use data‑driven approaches to strengthen policy development and service delivery.
  • Analyse ethical and legal dimensions of AI and identify success factors for responsible AI adoption in the public sector.
  • Develop a practical action plan to implement digital transformation initiatives in their home institutions.

Course Structure and Experience

The training programme is highly interactive and grounded in real‑world practice. Participants engage in:

  • Self‑assessments
  • Group discussions
  • Peer‑to‑peer learning
  • Collaborative exercises
  • Field visits to Dutch institutions leading in digital governance

Previous course visits included:

  • Ministry of Infrastructure and Water Management Participants explored how the ministry enhances online citizen participation in infrastructure projects.
  • Utrecht Data Walking Tour A hands‑on experience demonstrating how municipal data is used to improve public services.
  • Municipality of The Hague Visits to innovation teams to understand how data improves operational efficiency and service design.

Key Information

  • Course Dates: 16–20 November 2026
  • Location: The Hague, Netherlands
  • Course Fee: €2,950 (includes training materials, drinks, and warm lunches)
  • Discounts: Available for groups of three or more
  • Certificate: Participants receive a professional certificate from The Hague Academy
  • Reduced Fee: Available for professionals from DAC‑listed ODA‑recipient countries

Scholarship Opportunity

A fully funded scholarship is available for municipal professionals:

  • Under the age of 38
  • From countries on the DAC list of ODA‑recipient nations
  • Working in local government
  • Passionate about driving digital innovation

Application Deadline: 15 July 2026

Experts Involved

The course features leading experts in digital governance, including:

  • Dr. Dara Sherwani Acting Head of Information Science, American University of Kurdistan. Researches user trust in online content and predictive social media behaviours.
  • Shahenda ElNaggar Programme Manager at The Hague Academy, specialising in digital governance and public policy in the MENA region.
  • Jèron Perus Strategic Advisor at the Dutch National Office for Identity Data, responsible for safeguarding citizen data.

Practical Information

Participants can access additional details on:

  • Payment
  • Visa applications
  • Cancellation policies
  • Course brochure download

Apply here

For more information about this opportunity visit here

Discover more courses here

The post Digital Transformation in the Public Sector: A Comprehensive Training Programme by The Hague Academy for Local Governance appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Spirit of Togetherness Award 2026: Empowering Young Leaders Building Connection & Belonging
item.dc:creator: Sarah Kayiwa
item.description:
In a world rapidly transformed by digitalisation, mobility, and shifting social norms, the need for genuine human connection has never been more urgent. Communities everywhere are experiencing both new opportunities for interaction and rising challenges such as isolation, fragmentation, and loss of belonging. Against this backdrop, young leaders are stepping forward to reimagine how people […]

The post Spirit of Togetherness Award 2026: Empowering Young Leaders Building Connection & Belonging appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
In a world rapidly transformed by digitalisation, mobility, and shifting social norms, the need for genuine human connection has never been more urgent. Communities everywhere are experiencing both new opportunities for interaction and rising challenges such as isolation, fragmentation, and loss of belonging. Against this backdrop, young leaders are stepping forward to reimagine how people gather, share, and build meaningful relationships.

The Spirit of Togetherness Award 2026, presented by Pernod Ricard in partnership with One Young World, celebrates these changemakers. Building on the success of the WE Award 2025, this initiative recognises young innovators who are creating inclusive spaces, fostering cultural exchange, and strengthening social cohesion through the power of conviviality.

About the Spirit of Togetherness Award

Pernod Ricard defines conviviality as the powerful act of bringing people together—whether through shared meals, cultural traditions, conversations, or everyday moments that spark joy, trust, and understanding. This award honours young leaders who embody this spirit by designing initiatives that promote belonging and connection.

As communities evolve, young adults are increasingly becoming architects of inclusive environments—both physical and digital—where diversity is celebrated and every voice is valued. Their work demonstrates that connection is not accidental; it is intentionally created, nurtured, and sustained.

The Spirit of Togetherness Award 2026 recognises five outstanding young leaders whose initiatives are transforming how people come together.

What the Award Offers

Each of the five selected award recipients will receive:

  • €8,000 grant to expand and scale their initiative
  • Access to a global network of Conviviality Ambassadors
  • Opportunities for international visibility, collaboration, and impact amplification

This award is designed not only to support individual leaders but also to inspire a global movement centred on empathy, shared experiences, and human connection.

Focus Areas for Eligible Initiatives

Candidates must be leading an initiative that aligns with one or more of the following themes:

  1. Facilitating joyful, shared moments within communities, workplaces, or cultural spaces.
  2. Creating inclusive environments that bridge diverse groups—such as intergenerational, multicultural, or differently‑abled communities.
  3. Crafting everyday moments of cultural togetherness, rooted in local heritage, traditions, food, drink, and cultural expression.

These initiatives should demonstrate how conviviality can strengthen social cohesion, foster inclusion, and enrich cultural life.

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Must be at least 18 years old and meet the legal drinking age in their country of residence
  • Must be 35 years old or younger
  • Open to nationals of all countries
  • Must demonstrate:
    • Impact – measurable community engagement and positive outcomes
    • Inclusivity – commitment to bringing diverse people together
    • Scalability – potential for replication and growth

Additional consideration may be given to initiatives that incorporate environmental sustainability or innovation.

Application Details

  • Opening Date: 8 June 2026
  • Application Deadline: 20 July 2026
  • How to Apply: Visit the official One Young World application portal
  • Number of Awardees: 5

This award is a unique opportunity for young leaders to elevate their work, gain global recognition, and join a network of changemakers committed to building a more connected world.

Conclusion

The Spirit of Togetherness Award 2026 champions young leaders who are redefining how communities connect. By spotlighting their work, Pernod Ricard and One Young World aim to inspire a global shift toward more inclusive, empathetic, and convivial societies. The future of community-building lies in shared experiences—and this award empowers the leaders shaping that future.

Apply here

For more information about this opportunity visit here

Discover more award opportunities here

The post Spirit of Togetherness Award 2026: Empowering Young Leaders Building Connection & Belonging appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: IFRC Humanitarian Diplomacy Internship 2026 in New York, USA | Paid Internship ($110/Day) for Graduate Students
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
Are you passionate about humanitarian affairs, international relations, diplomacy, disaster response, and global policy? The International Federation of Red Cross and Red Crescent Societies (IFRC) has announced applications for its Humanitarian Diplomacy Internship 2026, offering an exceptional opportunity for graduate students and recent graduates to gain hands-on experience at the United Nations Headquarters in New […]

The post IFRC Humanitarian Diplomacy Internship 2026 in New York, USA | Paid Internship ($110/Day) for Graduate Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you passionate about humanitarian affairs, international relations, diplomacy, disaster response, and global policy? The International Federation of Red Cross and Red Crescent Societies (IFRC) has announced applications for its Humanitarian Diplomacy Internship 2026, offering an exceptional opportunity for graduate students and recent graduates to gain hands-on experience at the United Nations Headquarters in New York.

This paid internship allows participants to work directly with one of the world’s largest humanitarian organizations while contributing to humanitarian diplomacy, policy research, international advocacy, and global humanitarian initiatives. Interns will receive a stipend of USD $110 per day, gain exposure to the United Nations system, and build valuable professional networks within the humanitarian and international development sectors.

If you aspire to build a career with the United Nations, humanitarian organizations, international NGOs, or diplomatic institutions, this internship provides practical experience that will strengthen your professional profile.

About the International Federation of Red Cross and Red Crescent Societies (IFRC)

The International Federation of Red Cross and Red Crescent Societies (IFRC) is the world’s largest humanitarian network.

Working together with:

  • 191 National Red Cross and Red Crescent Societies
  • More than 16.5 million active volunteers
  • Humanitarian operations reaching approximately 160 million people worldwide

The IFRC works to improve the lives of vulnerable people through disaster response, health services, climate resilience, migration support, humanitarian diplomacy, community development, and emergency preparedness.

Its Delegation to the United Nations serves as the organization’s official representative at UN Headquarters and works closely with:

  • UN Member States
  • UN Agencies
  • International partners
  • Governments
  • Humanitarian organizations

The delegation also advocates for humanitarian priorities and ensures vulnerable populations remain at the center of global policy discussions.

Internship Overview

Position: Intern – Humanitarian Diplomacy

Organization:
International Federation of Red Cross and Red Crescent Societies (IFRC)

Location:
New York City, New York, USA

Work Format:
On-site

Internship Duration:

  • Start Date: August 31, 2026
  • End Date: December 31, 2026

Minimum Commitment:

  • 2–3 working days per week
  • Minimum duration of four months
  • Possible extension depending on organizational needs

Compensation:

  • USD $110 per day
  • Paid twice every month

Please note that internship payments are taxable unless exempt under applicable double-taxation agreements.

What Will Interns Gain?

Successful applicants will have the opportunity to:

  • Develop practical experience in humanitarian diplomacy.
  • Understand how the United Nations operates in practice.
  • Learn how international humanitarian policy is developed.
  • Gain exposure to global humanitarian negotiations.
  • Contribute to IFRC representation at the United Nations.
  • Strengthen expertise in disaster management, migration, health, humanitarian affairs, and international relations.
  • Build professional networks within the international humanitarian community.
  • Improve research, communication, policy analysis, and advocacy skills.

Internship Responsibilities

Interns will support the IFRC Delegation to the United Nations through a wide range of responsibilities.

1. Monitor UN Meetings and Processes

Interns will:

  • Follow discussions at the UN General Assembly.
  • Observe meetings of the Economic and Social Council.
  • Monitor Security Council discussions.
  • Prepare summaries and action points for IFRC teams.

Key policy topics include:

  • Humanitarian coordination
  • Global partnerships
  • Disaster risk reduction
  • Public health
  • Migration
  • Protection of vulnerable populations

2. Research and Policy Analysis

Interns will:

  • Conduct research on humanitarian issues.
  • Prepare analytical reports.
  • Support policy development.
  • Monitor emerging humanitarian challenges.

3. Draft Official Statements

Participants will assist with:

  • Preparing statements delivered to the UN General Assembly.
  • Editing policy documents.
  • Reviewing humanitarian messaging.
  • Supporting advocacy materials.

4. Support Humanitarian Negotiations

Interns will:

  • Observe UN negotiation processes.
  • Track developments in humanitarian resolutions.
  • Support advocacy efforts aimed at advancing humanitarian priorities.

5. Communications and Administrative Support

Responsibilities include:

  • Creating and maintaining databases.
  • Drafting correspondence.
  • Supporting digital communications.
  • Processing information requests.
  • Assisting with daily administrative tasks.

6. Uphold Humanitarian Principles

Interns are expected to work in accordance with the Fundamental Principles of the Red Cross and Red Crescent Movement throughout the internship.

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants should:

  • Be currently enrolled in graduate studies or have recently graduated.
  • Study or have studied:
    • International Relations
    • Humanitarian Assistance
    • Political Science
    • Or another closely related discipline.
  • Demonstrate strong interest in:
    • Humanitarian action
    • International development
    • Global policy
    • International relations.
  • Possess excellent written and verbal communication skills.
  • Have experience with communications and social media.
  • Be able to explain complex issues clearly.
  • Demonstrate attention to detail.
  • Work effectively in small teams.
  • Be flexible, organized, and capable of working independently.

Visa Requirements

Applicants must already possess:

  • A valid U.S. visa
  • A valid U.S. Social Security Number

The IFRC does not provide visa sponsorship or visa assistance.

Applicants are responsible for:

  • Travel expenses
  • Accommodation
  • Living costs
  • Visa arrangements

Internship Benefits

Selected interns will enjoy numerous professional benefits, including:

  • Paid internship at USD $110 per day.
  • Real-world experience working with the United Nations.
  • Exposure to humanitarian diplomacy.
  • Professional mentorship.
  • International networking opportunities.
  • Hands-on policy and advocacy experience.
  • Enhanced research and communication skills.
  • Opportunity to contribute to global humanitarian initiatives.
  • Potential internship extension.

How to Apply

Applicants must submit:

  • Resume (CV)
  • Cover Letter

The cover letter should clearly indicate:

  • Number of days available per week
  • Desired internship duration

Applications should be submitted via email to:

Newyork.delegation@ifrc.org

Only complete applications will be considered.

Application Deadline

Deadline: July 7, 2026 (11:59 PM Eastern Time)

Late or incomplete applications will not be accepted.

Why You Should Apply

The IFRC Humanitarian Diplomacy Internship is an outstanding opportunity for graduate students interested in humanitarian affairs, diplomacy, international policy, disaster management, and global development. Working alongside experienced humanitarian professionals at the United Nations offers valuable exposure that can open doors to future careers with international organizations, NGOs, governments, and multilateral institutions.

If you are committed to humanitarian action and aspire to make a global impact, this internship provides the knowledge, practical experience, and international exposure needed to advance your career.

For more information, visit the official website 

Discover more global paid internship on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post IFRC Humanitarian Diplomacy Internship 2026 in New York, USA | Paid Internship ($110/Day) for Graduate Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Mediterranean Futures Residency 2026 in Tunisia | Fully Funded International Residency for Artists, Designers & Creative Technologists
item.dc:creator: Stalin Daniel
item.description:
The Mediterranean Futures Residency 2026 is now officially open for applications, offering an exceptional fully funded international residency opportunity for artists, designers, architects, researchers, and creative technologists interested in exploring the intersection of cultural heritage, contemporary art, memory, and emerging technologies. Organized by EL SPACE in Tunisia, this prestigious two-month residency will take place from […]

The post Mediterranean Futures Residency 2026 in Tunisia | Fully Funded International Residency for Artists, Designers & Creative Technologists appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Mediterranean Futures Residency 2026 is now officially open for applications, offering an exceptional fully funded international residency opportunity for artists, designers, architects, researchers, and creative technologists interested in exploring the intersection of cultural heritage, contemporary art, memory, and emerging technologies.

Organized by EL SPACE in Tunisia, this prestigious two-month residency will take place from October 1 to November 30, 2026, in the culturally significant regions of Tunis, Sidi Bou Said, and Carthage. The residency is designed to support experimental artistic research, interdisciplinary collaboration, and critical inquiry into how technology is reshaping the understanding, preservation, and future of heritage.

A select cohort of only five international residents will be chosen through a competitive global open call.

About the Mediterranean Futures Residency 2026

The Mediterranean Futures Residency is an ambitious international initiative that brings together practitioners from across disciplines to investigate how heritage evolves in an age shaped by digital transformation, artificial intelligence, technological mediation, and changing cultural narratives.

Rather than treating heritage as a static archive of the past, the residency encourages participants to examine heritage as an evolving, contested, and future-oriented field of cultural production.

Residents will engage with:

  • Cultural memory
  • Architecture and archaeology
  • Archives and counter-archives
  • Oral histories and storytelling
  • Artificial intelligence and heritage
  • Digital fabrication
  • Contemporary artistic practice
  • Sound, image, and technological experimentation
  • Climate and environmental impacts on cultural identity
  • Decolonial narratives and networked culture

The program prioritizes experimentation, dialogue, speculative thinking, and process-driven research over polished exhibition-ready outputs.

Residency Location: Tunisia’s Historic and Cultural Landscape

The residency is situated within one of the Mediterranean region’s most historically rich environments.

Participants will be based in Tunis and work extensively in:

  • Sidi Bou Said
  • Carthage
  • Ennejma Ezzahra Palace
  • EL FABSPACE Lac
  • EL SPACE Creative Hub

Residents will have opportunities to immerse themselves in Tunisia’s layered Mediterranean, African, and Arab cultural histories while gaining access to contemporary digital fabrication tools and innovation infrastructure.

The residency creates a unique bridge between historical knowledge systems and emerging technologies.

Curatorial Focus of the Residency

The curatorial framework centers on the idea that heritage is not simply inherited from the past but actively constructed, interpreted, and transformed through contemporary culture and technological shifts.

Participants are encouraged to critically explore questions such as:

  • How do digital technologies reshape collective memory?
  • What happens when AI interacts with archives and historical narratives?
  • How can artists reinterpret architecture, craft traditions, and material culture?
  • What role does technology play in preserving or challenging heritage?
  • How can artistic practice create new relationships between history and future societies?

The residency strongly supports transdisciplinary and experimental approaches.

Areas of Interest

Applications may focus on a broad range of themes, including but not limited to:

Cultural & Historical Inquiry

  • Cultural memory and collective identity
  • Oral histories and storytelling
  • Public space and contested histories
  • Decolonial historical narratives
  • Archives and counter-archives

Art, Architecture & Spatial Practice

  • Architecture and spatial heritage
  • Urbanism and heritage preservation
  • Material culture and craftsmanship
  • Contemporary artistic interpretations of heritage

Technology & Innovation

  • Artificial intelligence and memory
  • Digital heritage and preservation
  • Interactive and digital works
  • Digital fabrication and prototyping
  • Technological mediation through sound and image

Environmental & Future-Oriented Research

  • Climate change impacts on local cultures
  • Speculative futures of heritage
  • Networked culture and emerging technologies

Who Can Apply?

The Mediterranean Futures Residency welcomes both emerging and established practitioners from diverse disciplines.

Eligible applicants include:

  • Visual artists
  • Photographers
  • Video artists
  • Sound artists
  • Designers
  • Architects
  • Digital and new media artists
  • Creative technologists
  • Independent researchers
  • Scholars
  • Hybrid and interdisciplinary practitioners

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • A strong artistic or research practice
  • Conceptual clarity
  • Critical engagement with heritage and technology
  • A compelling project proposal
  • Interest in interdisciplinary collaboration

What the Residency Covers

Selected residents will receive a comprehensive support package, including:

Fully Funded Accommodation

Participants will receive full accommodation throughout the two-month residency in Tunis.

Travel Support

The program provides travel assistance to and from Tunisia.

Meal Support

Residents will receive meal support during the residency period.

Production & Fabrication Support

Participants will gain access to advanced digital fabrication facilities, including:

  • 3D printing
  • Laser cutting
  • Digital fabrication equipment
  • Prototyping resources

Mentorship & Research Guidance

Residents will receive curatorial support and mentorship from invited specialists, researchers, and practitioners.

Institutional & Archival Access

Participants will have opportunities to engage with:

  • Cultural institutions
  • Heritage archives
  • Historical collections
  • Creative communities

International Cohort Experience

Residents will collaborate within a highly selective international interdisciplinary cohort.

Public Presentation Opportunities

The residency culminates in public presentations, exhibitions, conversations, and critical dialogues.

Residency Philosophy

The Mediterranean Futures Residency is grounded in the belief that artistic practice is a unique form of knowledge production capable of generating new understandings of culture, history, and collective life.

The residency emphasizes:

  • Research-based artistic inquiry
  • Intellectual experimentation
  • Speculative thinking
  • Process-oriented work
  • Interdisciplinary exchange
  • Creative risk-taking

Participants are encouraged to pursue ambitious and unresolved ideas rather than finalized commercial projects.

Possible Residency Outcomes

Projects developed during the residency may include:

  • Video and moving-image works
  • Sound installations and compositions
  • Digital and interactive artworks
  • Experimental archives
  • Fabricated objects and prototypes
  • Hybrid artistic formats
  • Research-based installations

Public Program & International Exchange

Throughout the residency, participants will contribute to:

  • Studio visits
  • Public talks
  • Artist presentations
  • Informal exchanges
  • Critical discussions
  • Collaborative workshops

The residency will also host invited curators, theorists, architects, and cultural practitioners from across the Mediterranean region and beyond.

Selection Process

Only five residents will be selected through an international review process conducted by an Advisory Committee.

Applications will be evaluated based on:

  1. Artistic excellence
  2. Conceptual originality
  3. Relevance to the curatorial framework
  4. Research quality
  5. Feasibility of the proposed project
  6. Potential contribution to the cohort
  7. Engagement with Tunisia’s cultural context

Shortlisted applicants may be invited for an interview or conversation before final selection.

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit all materials in English or French.

Required documents include:

  1. Curriculum Vitae (maximum 3 pages)
  2. Artist Biography (maximum 300 words)
  3. Statement of Practice (maximum 500 words)
  4. Project Proposal (maximum 1,000 words)
  5. Portfolio (up to 10 works)
  6. Preliminary Production & Resources Plan
  7. Optional links to websites, videos, or online work

All application materials must be submitted as PDF files.

Application Deadline

Application Deadline: July 19, 2026 (12 PM Tunis Time)

Residency Dates

  • Notification of Results: July 30, 2026
  • Residency Period: October 1 – November 30, 2026

About EL SPACE

EL SPACE is Tunisia’s first social innovation hub dedicated to empowering communities through creativity, innovation, and interdisciplinary collaboration.

Founded in 2015 following Tunisia’s democratic transition, EL SPACE has grown into one of North Africa’s most dynamic creative ecosystems.

Its network includes:

EL SPACE Lafayette

A coworking and civic innovation hub in downtown Tunis.

EL FABSPACE Lac

A digital fabrication laboratory equipped with:

  • 3D printers
  • Laser cutters
  • PCB milling machines
  • 3D scanners

Creative Hub Sidi Bou Said

A cultural innovation space located within the historic Ennejma Ezzahra Palace dedicated to art, craftsmanship, and design.

The Mediterranean Futures Residency combines all these spaces into a singular environment where historical heritage meets future-oriented artistic experimentation.

Applicants interested in contemporary art, digital culture, heritage research, speculative design, and interdisciplinary collaboration are strongly encouraged to apply.

How to Apply

Applications must be submitted via email to: aslema@elspace.co

Click HERE to learn more about the Mediterranean Futures Residency 2026 in Tunisia.

Explore more opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post Mediterranean Futures Residency 2026 in Tunisia | Fully Funded International Residency for Artists, Designers & Creative Technologists appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: People‑First AI Fund 2026: A Comprehensive Guide to the $50 Million Community-Centered AI Investment
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The OpenAI Foundation has announced a major expansion of its commitment to community-centered innovation with the launch of the 2026 People‑First AI Fund, backed by a $50 million investment to support nonprofits exploring how AI can strengthen their missions and uplift their communities. According to the document, “The OpenAI Foundation is committing $50 million in […]

The post People‑First AI Fund 2026: A Comprehensive Guide to the $50 Million Community-Centered AI Investment appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The OpenAI Foundation has announced a major expansion of its commitment to community-centered innovation with the launch of the 2026 People‑First AI Fund, backed by a $50 million investment to support nonprofits exploring how AI can strengthen their missions and uplift their communities. According to the document, “The OpenAI Foundation is committing $50 million in 2026 to support nonprofits engaging with AI to advance their missions and serve their communities.”

Applications are officially open until July 15, 2026 at 11:59 PM PT.

This article provides a broad, detailed, SEO‑optimized breakdown of the Fund’s purpose, focus areas, eligibility requirements, expectations, and application considerations for 2026.

Overview of the People‑First AI Fund 2026

The People‑First AI Fund was created to empower trusted, community-rooted nonprofits that are exploring how AI can reduce barriers to services, expand opportunity, and elevate community voice. The document notes that the Fund supports organizations “working at the intersection of innovation and public good” and aims to help nonprofits “engage with AI in ways that strengthen their missions and communities.”

In 2026, the Fund expands its scope based on learnings from the 2025 cycle, including updated focus areas and broader eligibility that now includes certain community foundations and regranting organizations.

2026 Focus Areas

The Fund will support nonprofits working across three major community-centered domains. Each area emphasizes practical, community-led engagement with AI.

1. Community Support Services

This focus area prioritizes nonprofits that help people navigate complex systems and access essential services. The document highlights priority areas such as “legal aid, public benefits, and disability and accessibility services.”

Organizations should be exploring how AI can:

  • Improve coordination and access to services
  • Reduce administrative burdens on frontline staff
  • Reach underserved or hard-to-reach populations
  • Support youth, rural communities, and underserved groups
  • Strengthen service delivery where systems are overstretched

2. Community Arts & Cultural Organizations

This category supports organizations that preserve cultural identity, foster creativity, and expand access to arts and cultural participation.

Eligible organizations include:

  • Local arts nonprofits
  • Museums
  • Libraries
  • Cultural centers
  • Community-based music and arts groups

AI-related goals may include:

  • Broadening participation in the arts
  • Reducing operational burdens
  • Documenting and sharing community stories
  • Preserving cultural expression and human creativity

3. Community Journalism & Media

This area supports organizations that strengthen local information ecosystems and help communities navigate AI in modern media environments.

Eligible organizations include:

  • Local media outlets
  • Nonprofit newsrooms
  • Public-interest media organizations
  • Journalism-adjacent nonprofits
  • Media literacy organizations

AI-related goals may include:

  • Expanding access to trusted local information
  • Increasing reach and accessibility of community journalism
  • Helping communities understand and engage with AI
  • Supporting reliable reporting in an evolving information landscape

Eligibility Criteria for 2026

To qualify, organizations must meet all of the following requirements:

1. U.S.-Based 501(c)(3) Public Charity

Organizations must be U.S.-based and compliant with 501(c)(3) regulations.

2. Primarily U.S.-Focused Operations

Work must be conducted primarily within the 50 U.S. states or Washington, D.C.

3. Budget Requirements

  • Annual operating budget between $500,000 and $10 million
  • Priority for organizations between $1–8 million
  • Regranting organizations may have budgets up to $15 million (excluding pass-through grantmaking)

4. Standalone Organizations Only

The Fund will not consider:

  • Programs within larger institutions
  • University-affiliated centers
  • Think tanks
  • Fiscally sponsored programs

However, it will consider:

  • Local chapters of national organizations with standalone 501(c)(3) status
  • Select regranting organizations with strong community trust and grantmaking infrastructure

Competitive Applicant Characteristics

Strong applicants will demonstrate:

  • Genuine interest in exploring AI, even without prior experience
  • Deep community trust and long-standing relationships
  • Community co-creation and people-centered programming
  • Clear alignment with one of the three focus areas

The document emphasizes that the Fund is “platform-agnostic: use of specific AI tools or providers is not required and will not affect funding decisions.”

Regranting Institutions: Additional Requirements

Regranting organizations must:

  • Be U.S.-based 501(c)(3) public charities
  • Have operating budgets under $15 million
  • Demonstrate strong financial controls and grantmaking infrastructure
  • Support community-rooted initiatives aligned with the Fund’s focus areas
  • Regrant only to organizations (not individuals)

Grant Funding and Use of Funds

The Fund provides unrestricted grants to eligible nonprofits. Regranting organizations, however, will receive restricted funds with additional reporting requirements.

Key notes:

  • No requirement to use OpenAI products
  • No free OpenAI credits included
  • Collaborative projects may be proposed
  • Indirect cost policies apply depending on organization type

Grant Expectations & Timeline

    • Application Deadline: July 15, 2026 at 11:59 PM PT
    • Applicants will be notified after review
    • Grant periods and reporting requirements vary by organization type
    • Late submissions are not accepted

 

Apply here

For more information about this opportunity visit here

Discover more grant opportunities here

 

The post People‑First AI Fund 2026: A Comprehensive Guide to the $50 Million Community-Centered AI Investment appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: WIC Global Youth Leaders Program 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for Emerging Digital, AI, and Internet Leaders Worldwide
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
Applications Open for the World Internet Conference Global Youth Leadership Program (GYLP) 2026 Young innovators, digital leaders, internet governance experts, technology entrepreneurs, researchers, policymakers, and changemakers from around the world now have an exciting opportunity to join one of the most influential international youth leadership initiatives in the digital sector. The World Internet Conference (WIC) […]

The post WIC Global Youth Leaders Program 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for Emerging Digital, AI, and Internet Leaders Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications Open for the World Internet Conference Global Youth Leadership Program (GYLP) 2026

Young innovators, digital leaders, internet governance experts, technology entrepreneurs, researchers, policymakers, and changemakers from around the world now have an exciting opportunity to join one of the most influential international youth leadership initiatives in the digital sector.

The World Internet Conference (WIC) has officially opened applications for the 2026 Global Youth Leadership Program (GYLP), a prestigious global platform designed to identify, connect, and empower the next generation of leaders shaping the future of the internet, artificial intelligence, digital governance, and technological innovation.

Selected Global Youth Leaders will gain access to international forums, high-level networking opportunities, leadership development activities, research collaborations, and the opportunity to attend and speak at the renowned World Internet Conference Wuzhen Summit in China.

Applications are open until July 24, 2026.

About the World Internet Conference (WIC)

The World Internet Conference has become one of the world’s most influential international platforms for dialogue, cooperation, and governance in cyberspace.

Since the inaugural Wuzhen Summit in 2014, the conference has successfully convened global leaders from:

  • Governments
  • International organizations
  • Technology companies
  • Academic institutions
  • Research organizations
  • Industry associations
  • Civil society organizations

Each year, more than 1,000 high-level representatives gather to discuss critical issues surrounding:

  • Internet governance
  • Artificial intelligence
  • Digital transformation
  • Cybersecurity
  • Digital economy development
  • International technological cooperation

In July 2022, the World Internet Conference was formally established as an international organization headquartered in Beijing, China.

The organization seeks to promote:

  • Global digital cooperation
  • Shared internet governance
  • Inclusive technological development
  • International collaboration
  • Digital innovation
  • Sustainable cyberspace development

What is the Global Youth Leadership Program (GYLP)?

Launched in 2023, the Global Youth Leadership Program is one of the flagship initiatives of the World Internet Conference.

The program identifies outstanding young professionals and leaders working across internet-related fields and provides them with opportunities to contribute to global discussions about the future of technology and digital governance.

Over the past three years:

  • 56 Global Youth Leaders from 38 countries and regions have participated.
  • Participants have attended the Wuzhen Summit Youth Forum.
  • Youth leaders have engaged directly with some of the most influential figures in global technology and internet governance.
  • The 2025 program attracted applicants from nearly 120 countries and regions.

The initiative aims to create a long-term international network of youth leaders capable of driving positive digital transformation worldwide.

Core Pillars of the Program

1. Forum

Participants will take part in the Global Youth Leadership Forum during the WIC Wuzhen Summit.

The forum provides opportunities to:

  • Speak on major internet issues.
  • Share innovative perspectives.
  • Engage with global experts.
  • Influence discussions on digital governance.

2. Training

The program organizes leadership training and field research opportunities around the world.

Participants benefit from:

  • Expert lectures
  • Leadership development
  • International study visits
  • Skills enhancement programs
  • Global networking opportunities

3. Research

Youth leaders contribute to research initiatives focused on:

  • Internet governance
  • Artificial intelligence
  • Digital technology
  • Digital economy
  • Internet policy
  • Emerging technologies

Research outcomes may contribute to international discussions, policy recommendations, and governance frameworks.

Benefits of Becoming a WIC Global Youth Leader

Selected participants receive a wide range of benefits.

Official Recognition

Participants receive an official Global Youth Leader Certificate issued by the World Internet Conference.

Attend the 2026 WIC Wuzhen Summit

Global Youth Leaders will be invited to:

  • Attend the summit as official guests.
  • Speak at the Youth Forum.
  • Engage directly with international leaders.

Travel Support

Eligible participants may receive support covering:

  • Round-trip airfare
  • Accommodation
  • Meals and catering

Global Networking

Participants gain access to high-level networking opportunities with:

  • Government representatives
  • International organizations
  • Technology executives
  • Academic experts
  • Researchers
  • Industry leaders

Leadership Opportunities

Global Youth Leaders may also:

  • Organize workshops and side events.
  • Participate in future WIC activities.
  • Lead youth initiatives.
  • Build international collaborations.

Career Development

Participants receive:

  • Priority consideration for WIC internships.
  • Priority access to job opportunities within the organization.

Youth Community Membership

Outstanding finalists who are not ultimately selected still receive:

  • Nominee Certificates
  • Access to the WIC Youth Community
  • Networking opportunities
  • Priority internship access

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must:

  1. Support the mission and vision of the World Internet Conference.
  2. Be under 45 years of age.
  3. Demonstrate achievements in internet-related fields.
  4. Possess an international perspective.
  5. Show interdisciplinary thinking.
  6. Have strong communication and presentation skills.

Preferred Professional Backgrounds

Policy and Governance

Applicants may be:

  • Government officials
  • Policy analysts
  • Digital governance experts
  • International organization professionals

Business and Entrepreneurship

Applicants may be:

  • Startup founders
  • Technology entrepreneurs
  • Digital business leaders
  • Innovation professionals

Research and Academia

Applicants may have expertise in:

  • Artificial Intelligence
  • Big Data
  • Internet Governance
  • Emerging Technologies
  • Digital Policy

Public Interest and Civil Society

Applicants may work in:

  • NGOs
  • Digital inclusion initiatives
  • Technology-for-good projects
  • Sustainable development programs

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit:

1. Recommendation Letter

A recommendation from:

  • A professional institution
  • Academic institution
  • Recognized industry expert

2. Supporting Documents

Examples include:

  • Awards
  • Publications
  • Research outputs
  • Patents
  • Professional achievements

3. Video Submission

Applicants must submit:

  • A video presentation
  • Written transcript

The video should discuss:

  • Views on the WIC Global Youth Leaders Initiative
  • Proposed activities or projects
  • Vision for digital development and internet governance

APPLY HERE

Important Dates

Application Deadline

July 24, 2026
17:00 (GMT+8)

Review Process

By the end of August 2026

Selection Notifications

By the end of September 2026

Wuzhen Summit Participation

Tentatively scheduled for November 2026

Visit HERE to learn more about the WIC Global Youth Leaders Program 2026

Additional Opportunity: Global Logo Design Competition

Alongside the fellowship application, WIC is also inviting global creators to design the official logo of the Global Youth Leadership Program.

Who Can Participate?

Anyone worldwide.

Submission Deadline

July 31, 2026
17:00 (UTC+8)

Benefits for Selected Designers

  • Global recognition
  • Official adoption of the logo
  • Commemorative gifts
  • Invitation to attend the 2026 WIC Wuzhen Summit Youth Forum

This provides a unique opportunity for designers and creative professionals to contribute to a major international youth initiative.

Click HERE to read more and apply for the Global call for logo designs for WIC Global Youth Leadership Program

Why You Should Apply

The WIC Global Youth Leadership Program stands out as one of the most prestigious global youth leadership initiatives focused on internet governance, AI, digital transformation, and technology policy.

Participants gain:

  • International visibility
  • Global networking
  • Leadership development
  • Speaking opportunities
  • Research collaboration
  • Career advancement
  • Access to influential digital governance discussions

For young leaders committed to shaping the future of technology and the internet, this program offers a rare opportunity to engage directly with decision-makers and contribute to global conversations that will define the digital age.

Conclusion

As artificial intelligence, digital governance, cybersecurity, and internet technologies continue to reshape societies worldwide, the need for globally minded youth leaders has never been greater.

The World Internet Conference Global Youth Leadership Program 2026 provides a powerful platform for emerging leaders to engage with policymakers, researchers, entrepreneurs, and innovators while helping shape the future of cyberspace.

If you are passionate about technology, governance, innovation, AI, digital inclusion, or internet policy, this fully funded opportunity could be your gateway to becoming part of a global network of future digital leaders.

Interested applicants are encouraged to prepare their materials early and submit their applications before the July 24, 2026 deadline.

Discover more global Fellowship, Conference and Leadership Development opportunities on OFY.

The post WIC Global Youth Leaders Program 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for Emerging Digital, AI, and Internet Leaders Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: WHO African Region Opens Applications for 2026 Roster of Consultants on NTD Health Systems Integration
item.dc:creator: Stalin Daniel
item.description:
The World Health Organization (WHO) African Region has officially opened applications for its 2026 Roster of Consultants on Neglected Tropical Diseases (NTD) Health Systems Integration. This prestigious consultancy opportunity is designed for experienced public health and health systems professionals who are passionate about strengthening healthcare systems and advancing sustainable disease control strategies across Africa. The […]

The post WHO African Region Opens Applications for 2026 Roster of Consultants on NTD Health Systems Integration appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The World Health Organization (WHO) African Region has officially opened applications for its 2026 Roster of Consultants on Neglected Tropical Diseases (NTD) Health Systems Integration. This prestigious consultancy opportunity is designed for experienced public health and health systems professionals who are passionate about strengthening healthcare systems and advancing sustainable disease control strategies across Africa.

The consultancy roster will support the WHO Regional Office for Africa through the Expanded Special Project for Elimination of Neglected Tropical Diseases (ESPEN). Selected experts may be called upon to provide technical assistance to countries across multiple domains including health financing, supply chain systems, integrated service delivery, digital health systems, surveillance, and health information management.

This is a major opportunity for global public health professionals, consultants, epidemiologists, health informatics experts, supply chain specialists, and health systems strengthening practitioners who want to contribute to large-scale health transformation initiatives in low- and middle-income countries.

About WHO ESPEN and the Initiative

The Expanded Special Project for Elimination of Neglected Tropical Diseases (ESPEN) is part of the Disease Prevention and Control Cluster within the WHO African Region.

ESPEN works to help African countries achieve the goals of the WHO NTD Roadmap 2021–2030 by supporting sustainable and country-led approaches to eliminating neglected tropical diseases.

The initiative focuses heavily on:

  • Strengthening national health systems
  • Improving integrated disease control strategies
  • Enhancing surveillance systems
  • Supporting health financing sustainability
  • Integrating NTD programmes into routine healthcare systems
  • Promoting data-driven decision-making
  • Supporting community-centered healthcare interventions

As countries move away from standalone disease campaigns toward integrated national health systems, WHO is now seeking highly qualified consultants who can support this transition.

Purpose of the Consultant Roster

The main purpose of the roster is to create a pool of pre-qualified technical experts who can be rapidly deployed to support WHO Country Offices and national governments across Africa.

Consultants may support:

  1. Country readiness assessments
  2. Health systems integration planning
  3. Technical implementation support
  4. Monitoring and evaluation activities
  5. Documentation of country experiences and best practices
  6. Development of implementation tools and frameworks
  7. Capacity building and technical training
  8. Disease surveillance strengthening
  9. Health financing and sustainability planning

Being included in the roster does not guarantee immediate employment, but it allows WHO to mobilize consultants quickly when country-specific assignments become available.

Areas of Technical Expertise Required

WHO is seeking experts across several specialized domains.

1. Supply Chain and Medicines Management

Consultants in this area may support:

  • Integration of preventive chemotherapy medicines into national supply chain systems
  • Alignment with national medicines policies and essential medicines lists
  • Integration with electronic logistics management information systems (eLMIS)
  • Monitoring stock management, wastage, expiry tracking, and reconciliation systems
  • Strengthening campaign logistics and last-mile distribution systems

2. Health Information Systems and Digital Integration

WHO is particularly interested in professionals experienced in:

  • DHIS2 implementation and optimization
  • National HMIS integration
  • Digital health interoperability
  • Data governance systems
  • Mapping NTD data flows within national health systems
  • Alignment of NTD reporting systems with national digital health strategies

Consultants may work directly with Ministries of Health and WHO Country Offices to strengthen national digital health architecture.

3. Integrated Service Delivery

Experts may support:

  • Integrated health campaign planning
  • School-based and community-based service delivery models
  • Primary healthcare integration
  • Transition from campaign-driven models to routine healthcare delivery systems
  • Multi-disease service delivery approaches

4. Integrated Disease Surveillance

This technical area focuses on:

  • Integration of NTD surveillance into Integrated Disease Surveillance and Response (IDSR) systems
  • Post-elimination surveillance frameworks
  • Sentinel surveillance systems
  • Use of routine health data for outbreak response and disease monitoring

5. Health Financing and Sustainability

Consultants may contribute to:

  • National budgeting and planning integration
  • Domestic resource mobilization strategies
  • Sustainability planning for NTD interventions
  • Donor transition strategies
  • Financial analysis and policy development

Typical Consultancy Assignments

Selected consultants may be engaged in a wide range of short-term technical assignments including:

  • Country readiness assessments
  • Multi-country technical reviews
  • Health systems mapping exercises
  • Development of implementation roadmaps
  • Capacity-building workshops
  • Technical consultations and validation meetings
  • Policy analysis and strategic planning
  • Development of operational tools and frameworks
  • Data analysis and reporting
  • Knowledge product development
  • Documentation of lessons learned and best practices

Assignments may be remote, in-country, or hybrid depending on programme requirements.

Required Qualifications

Educational Requirements

Applicants should possess:

  • An advanced university degree (Master’s level or higher) in:
    • Public Health
    • Health Systems
    • Epidemiology
    • Health Informatics
    • Pharmacy
    • Supply Chain Management
    • Or another related field

Equivalent combinations of education and professional experience may also be considered.

Professional Experience Requirements

Applicants must have:

  • At least seven (7) years of progressively responsible professional experience
  • Strong experience in health systems strengthening or public health programmes
  • Experience working in low- and middle-income country settings
  • Proven collaboration with Ministries of Health
  • Experience supporting integration of disease-specific programmes into national systems

Experience in the following systems is highly valued:

  • DHIS2
  • HMIS
  • eLMIS
  • IDSR
  • Integrated digital health systems

Desirable Qualifications

WHO also values applicants with:

  • Experience working directly with NTD programmes
  • Monitoring and evaluation expertise
  • Multi-country or regional assessment experience
  • Experience documenting country case studies and best practices
  • Knowledge of preventive chemotherapy programmes
  • Strong analytical and report-writing skills

Language Requirements

Essential

Applicants must demonstrate expert knowledge of at least one of the following languages:

  • English
  • French
  • Portuguese

Desirable

Knowledge of additional UN official languages is considered an advantage.

WHO Core Competencies

Successful consultants are expected to demonstrate:

  • Teamwork
  • Communication skills
  • Partnership building
  • Respect for diversity and inclusion
  • Results-oriented work ethic

Contract Duration and Work Modality

Key consultancy details include:

  • Contract duration may vary between 1 and 11 months
  • Engagements are deliverable-based
  • Assignments may be:
    • Remote
    • In-country
    • Hybrid/blended

Remuneration will depend on the scope of work and available funding.

Important Deadline

Application Deadline: 6 July 2026

Applicants are strongly encouraged to apply early to avoid last-minute technical issues.

Important Notes for Applicants

  • Inclusion in the roster does not guarantee a contract.
  • WHO may use this roster to identify candidates for similar future consultancies.
  • A written assessment may be conducted during screening.
  • Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.
  • WHO strongly encourages applications from women and nationals of underrepresented Member States.
  • WHO maintains strict ethical standards and zero tolerance for harassment or abuse.

Why This Opportunity Matters

This consultancy roster represents an important opportunity for experienced public health professionals to contribute directly to health systems transformation across Africa.

Selected experts may help countries:

  • Improve healthcare delivery
  • Strengthen surveillance systems
  • Enhance access to medicines
  • Build sustainable health financing systems
  • Accelerate progress toward eliminating neglected tropical diseases

For professionals interested in global health consulting, health systems strengthening, digital health integration, epidemiology, or international public health policy, this WHO consultancy roster provides an exceptional platform to work on high-impact regional initiatives.

Learn More & Apply

Click HERE to learn more and apply for the WHO African Region Applications for 2026 Roster of Consultants on NTD Health Systems Integration.

Explore more opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post WHO African Region Opens Applications for 2026 Roster of Consultants on NTD Health Systems Integration appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Launch Your STEM Career with NASA: Apply Now for the JPL Year-Round Internship Program 2025
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
Aspiring scientists, engineers, and technologists—your gateway to hands-on experience at NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL) is open year-round. Whether you’re pursuing an undergraduate or graduate degree in a STEM field, the JPL Year-Round Internship Program offers a unique opportunity to work alongside world-class researchers and contribute to groundbreaking space and Earth science missions. What Is […]

The post Launch Your STEM Career with NASA: Apply Now for the JPL Year-Round Internship Program 2025 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Aspiring scientists, engineers, and technologists—your gateway to hands-on experience at NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL) is open year-round. Whether you’re pursuing an undergraduate or graduate degree in a STEM field, the JPL Year-Round Internship Program offers a unique opportunity to work alongside world-class researchers and contribute to groundbreaking space and Earth science missions.

What Is the JPL Year-Round Internship Program?

The Jet Propulsion Laboratory, managed by Caltech on behalf of NASA, is home to some of the most innovative planetary exploration and Earth observation missions. The JPL Year-Round Internship Program is designed to foster the next generation of leaders in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics by offering immersive, real-world experiences throughout the academic year and summer.

Through this program, selected interns are paired with JPL scientists and engineers, gaining not just practical exposure but also invaluable mentorship. These projects are carefully designed to align with the interns’ academic backgrounds while contributing meaningfully to JPL’s active missions.

Key Features of the Program

1. Flexible Timing
Internships are available throughout the year—fall, spring, and summer—making it easier for students to integrate the experience into their academic schedules. Interns can apply for part-time or full-time positions depending on their availability and institutional policies.

2. Paid Opportunities
Interns receive a weekly monetary stipend. For full-time, in-person participants whose universities are located more than 50 miles from JPL, a supplemental housing and travel allowance is also provided.

3. Enrichment Activities
Beyond project work, interns can attend enrichment sessions including lectures by NASA scientists, facility tours, career development workshops, and networking events. These experiences help broaden understanding of the space sector and the science-policy interface.

4. Personalized Mentorship
Each intern is matched with a mentor who supervises their work and provides guidance on both technical challenges and career planning. This one-on-one mentorship is a core component of the learning experience.

Who Is Eligible?

To qualify for the JPL Year-Round Internship Program, applicants must meet the following criteria:

  • Be currently enrolled in an accredited U.S. college or university as an undergraduate or graduate student in a STEM-related program (science, technology, engineering, or mathematics).

  • Maintain a minimum GPA of 3.0, with a preferred GPA of 3.5 or above.

  • Be a U.S. citizen or lawful permanent resident (LPR).

  • Be able to commit to a minimum 10-week internship for their first term. (Subsequent terms are more flexible.)

Importantly, applicants must be authorized by their university to pursue off-campus, independent study or internship during the academic year or summer.

How to Apply

Applications are submitted online through the JPL internship portal. The process includes:

  • Filling out the digital application form.

  • Uploading a current resume in PDF, DOC, or TXT format.

  • Submitting an unofficial transcript.

After submission, the Education Office at JPL screens applications for eligibility. If approved, applicants become visible to potential mentors who are looking for student collaborators on specific projects. Those selected as finalists may be contacted for an interview by their prospective mentors. Offers are extended based on mutual interest and project fit.

Why Apply?

Participating in the JPL Year-Round Internship Program is not just an educational milestone—it’s a career-launching opportunity. Interns become part of the NASA mission, gaining insight into space exploration, Earth science, and cutting-edge robotics. They work on meaningful projects that have the potential to fly on spacecraft, influence mission design, or impact how we understand our planet and beyond.

Past interns have contributed to missions like Mars Perseverance, Europa Clipper, and Earth-observing satellites. Many have transitioned into full-time roles at NASA centers or continued their studies with stronger research portfolios and networks.

to learn more and apply, click here

Discover more global internship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post Launch Your STEM Career with NASA: Apply Now for the JPL Year-Round Internship Program 2025 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
